query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the key findings:

**1. Extension beyond July 31, 2026?** — **No formal decree had been signed as of July 1, 2026.** The current full gasoline export ban runs April 1–July 31, 2026 (decree signed April 2, 2026, government.ru/news/58316). The government *signaled* on June 23 it was "seriously considering" extending bans "until the end of the year," and on June 30 Putin reaffirmed the existing ban and set up a daily-monitoring headquarters under Novak. A separate June 28 measure concerns the zero export *duty* (not the ban) being extended via the EAEU — distinct from the ban itself.

**2. Drone strikes & shortages (late June 2026)** — A record campaign: 25+ refinery strikes since March, 16 hit in May. Refining fell below 4 million bpd (21-year low), ~2.14 million bpd idle, ~13 refineries halted (~40% of capacity). Shortages span 53 regions; Crimea halted public retail sales; prices up 30–40% YTD; reserves ~1.7M tons (down 4% YoY); Russia exploring imports.

**3. Historical announcement timing** — Russia announces extensions **very close to deadlines, typically 0–4 days before expiry**: Aug 27, 2025 (~4 days before Aug 31), Sep 30, 2025 (day-of), Dec 27, 2025 (~4 days before Dec 31). Novak typically gives "we will extend" signals ~1 week ahead, with the formal decree in the final days — meaning any extension beyond July 31, 2026 would most plausibly land in late July 2026.

sources.snippet: Decision to extend zero export duty past July 31 expected from EEC next week; gasoline exchange norm cut from 15% to 10%.

sources.snippet: June 30, 2026: Putin reaffirmed complete temporary gasoline+kerosene ban; special headquarters under Novak; import negotiations.

sources.snippet: June 30: Peskov confirms import talks; ban extended until July 31; FAS/Energy Ministry propose cutting exchange minimum to 10%.

sources.snippet: Full gasoline export ban April 1–July 31, 2026; aviation kerosene ban until Nov 30, 2026; queues in 20+ regions.

sources.snippet: 53 regions affected; refining below 4 million bpd (21-year low); 2.14 million bpd idle; AI-92 +30%, AI-95 +33%, diesel +40%.

sources.snippet: 16 refineries hit in May (8 of 10 largest); Reuters: Russia plans gasoline imports via western port.

sources.snippet: Over two dozen strikes since March; Moscow refinery hit twice; Ukraine targets 10-15 largest modern refineries.

sources.snippet: Sep 30, 2025: decree prolongs gasoline export ban to Dec 31 (government.ru/news/56377).

sources.snippet: Dec 27, 2025: gasoline export ban extended through February 28, 2026.

sources.snippet: 2024 ban first imposed March 1, 2024; producer ban lifted early; context for ban history.

sources.title: Russia may permit Euro-2 standard gasoline
sources.snippet: Draft decree to allow Euro-2 to Euro-4 fuel production/import until July 2027 amid crisis; Putin called shortage 'non-critical'.

key_facts: As of July 1, 2026, NO formal decree extending Russia's gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 had been signed or published; the ban currently runs April 1–July 31, 2026 (decree signed April 2, 2026, government.ru/news/58316).

On June 23, 2026 the Russian government said it was 'seriously considering' extending gasoline/diesel/jet-fuel export bans 'until the end of the year' to avoid domestic shortages — a signal, not an enacted measure.

On June 30, 2026 Putin reaffirmed the existing complete gasoline+jet-fuel export ban and created a daily-monitoring headquarters led by Novak; the Kremlin (Peskov) confirmed Russia is negotiating petroleum-product imports.

A separate June 28, 2026 measure concerns the zero export DUTY (tariff) on gasoline — Russia asked the Eurasian Economic Commission to extend it through end-2026, with a decision expected 'next week'; this is distinct from the export ban.
Russian refining fell below 4 million bpd in early June 2026 (lowest in 21 years); roughly 2.14 million bpd (~one-third of capacity) was idle per Energy Intelligence.
Novaya Gazeta Europe reported 13 refineries halted, affecting ~40% of refining, 41% of gasoline production, and 44% of diesel output as of late June 2026.
Fuel purchase restrictions were in place across 53 Russian regions plus occupied territories; Crimea/Sevastopol halted retail public sales on June 21, 2026.
Year-to-date wholesale price rises: AI-92 +30%, AI-95 +33%, diesel +40% (record 75,900 rub/ton); aviation fuel hit a record 113,000 rub/ton.
Putin said gasoline reserves stood at ~1.7 million tons in late June 2026, down ~4% vs June 2025; Russia began drawing reserves and exploring imports.
Historical extension announcements came very close to deadlines: Aug 27, 2025 (to Sep 30, ~4 days before); Sep 30, 2025 (to Dec 31, day-of); Dec 27, 2025 (to Feb 28, 2026, ~4 days before).
Pattern: Novak typically gives 'we are considering / will extend' signals about a week before the deadline, with the formal cabinet decree published in the final 0–4 days before expiry.
